item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents reject automatic priority for society over individual rights from their first turns through their final turns. Both acknowledge narrow public-health, safety, order, or harm-prevention limits, but those are framed as exceptions under rights-first constraints rather than movement toward supporting the statement. Agent A briefly intensifies in turn 3, but the text preserves the same core position, so no flip is recorded.
